title: cirsium lanceolatum var. virens
intro: Cirsium lanceolatum var. virens, also known as the green lanceleaf thistle, is a perennial plant species in the family Asteraceae, characterized by its lance-shaped leaves and vibrant green foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Cirsium lanceolatum var. virens include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential antiviral and antibacterial activities. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a tea or infused in oil to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
